question: I need to understand the rules for proeftijd (probation period) in Dutch employment contracts.

expectations.evidence: Art. 7:652 BW is the central article of the entire output. Full Dutch text reproduced with all 6 lids. Article cited as '7:652 BW' throughout. BWB identifier BWBR0005290 provided.

expectations.pattern: one month/two month/één maand/twee maand/1 maand/2 maand
expectations.evidence: Both duration limits explicitly stated: '1 month (één maand)' for fixed-term contracts shorter than 2 years; '2 months (twee maanden)' for fixed-term contracts of 2 years or longer and open-ended contracts. Table also shows limits. Dutch terms used: 'twee maanden', 'één maand'.

Written requirement explicitly stated: 'The probation period MUST be agreed in writing (schriftelijk)'. Art. 7:652 lid 2 Dutch text: 'De proeftijd moet schriftelijk worden overeengekomen.' Practical section: 'A proeftijd agreed only verbally is nietig.'

Full disclaimer at end: 'IMPORTANT — NOT LEGAL ADVICE. This legislation lookup is generated by an AI system and is provided for general informational purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice...'

quality_notes: This is an exemplary legislation lookup output. Legal accuracy is excellent: the full Art. 7:652 BW text is correctly reproduced with all 6 lids, all duration limits are accurate, the nietigheid vs. vernietigbaarheid distinction is correctly made (a void proeftijd is nietig by operation of law, not merely voidable). The EU Directive 2019/1152 implementation note is accurate and important — the ban on proeftijd for contracts shorter than 6 months (Art. 7:652 lid 4 sub d) was indeed added by the 2022 implementation law. The CAO deviation mechanism (Art. 7:652 lid 6 BW allowing up to 3 months via CAO) is correctly stated. The ketenregeling interaction (Art. 7:668a BW) is correctly flagged. Completeness is outstanding: the output covers duration by contract type, the written requirement, all four grounds for automatic nullity, termination consequences, CAO deviations, practical pitfalls (6 identified), case law notes, recent changes (2022 EU Directive), and pending ZZP reform. The practical table is an excellent addition for quick reference. Actionability: the practical pitfalls section is particularly valuable, identifying the 6 most common employer mistakes with specific legal consequences. A user can immediately take concrete steps from this output. Citation depth: Art. 7:652 (all lids), Art. 7:610, 7:610a, 7:653, 7:655, 7:662, 7:668a, 7:669, 7:671, 7:628 BW, BWBR0005290, Directive 2019/1152/EU. Cross-referencing: references arbeidsrecht skill context, CAO system, Wet gelijke behandeling, Awgb anti-discrimination law, AVV (algemeen verbindend verklaring) mechanism, ZZP/VBAR reform. The BWB identifier (BWBR0005290) is correctly provided with verification URL.
